title: Use switch instead of if-else chain to compare a variable against multiple cases
description: When comparing a single variable against multiple constant values, using a switch statement or expression is clearer and less error-prone than a long if-else chain. Switch statements improve readability and maintainability, and can help prevent mistakes such as missing or duplicated conditions.
recommendation: Refactor if-else chains that compare the same variable against multiple values into a switch statement or expression.
impact: Improves code clarity and reduces the risk of errors in conditional logic.

checks.message: Refactor if-else chains that compare the same variable against multiple values into a switch statement or expression.
